message:
test(fnp-python): k74v.8 adversarial error-surface parity test

Adds `module_k74v_8_adversarial_error_surface_matches_numpy` — an
adversarial conformance test that exercises error surfaces for 8 of
the 31 k74v.8 passthrough wrappers. For each case, calls both
fnp_python.<name>(bad_input) and numpy.<name>(bad_input) and asserts
that when numpy raises, fnp_python raises the SAME exception type
(per /testing-conformance-harnesses skill's "Error handling
divergences are the most dangerous" anti-pattern).

Coverage:
- argmax / argmin on empty array → ValueError both sides
- dot with shape mismatch (3-vec vs 4-vec) → ValueError both sides
- matmul with incompatible ranks (1-D vs 2-D) → ValueError both sides
- einsum with malformed subscript ("abc" on 1-D array) → ValueError
- trace on 1-D array (needs ≥2-D) → ValueError both sides
- zeros / ones with negative shape → ValueError both sides

No divergences found — all 8 error types match. This confirms the
variadic (*args, **kwargs) passthrough pattern preserves error
semantics through pyo3's Py<PyAny> boundary. A future kwargs-specific
case (e.g. axis out-of-bounds, dtype-casting errors) can extend this
same harness.

Verified via:
  export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc
  cargo test -p fnp-python --lib module_k74v_8_adversarial
1 passed, 0 failed, finished in 0.10s.

Review-mode cycle 3 workspace state (commit 1e32582 + 4 beads) :
  cargo test --workspace: 3721 passed / 0 failed / 1 ignored
  cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: 0 errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
